entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A nutri-cereal grown predominantly by subsistence farmers in semi-arid regions of India and Africa

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country where pearl millet hybrids are a dominant cultivar type

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where pearl millet is grown

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Environmental regions where pearl millet is predominantly grown

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A system used to produce hybrids in pearl millet

entities.name: HYBRIDS
entities.description: Cultivars produced by crossing isogenic females with male parents

entities.name: ISONUCLEAR ALLOPLASMIC CMS
entities.description: Specific cytoplasmic systems A1, A4, and A5 used to generate hybrids

entities.name: AGRONOMIC PERFORMANCE
entities.description: The overall agricultural productivity and characteristics of pearl millet hybrids

entities.name: SPLIT-SPLIT-PLOT DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used to evaluate the hybrids

entities.name: CONTRASTING SEASONS
entities.description: Environmental variations used to test the stability of hybrids

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: 1000-GRAIN WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of 1000 grains, a component of grain yield

entities.name: MULTILOCATION TESTING
entities.description: The process of testing hybrids across different locations to ensure yield stability

entities.name: GENETIC BACKGROUNDS
entities.description: The diverse genetic makeup of parents contributing to hybrid traits
